title: Add column types system, refs #2664
overview: This PR introduces a column types system: a way to annotate specific table columns with semantic types (url, email, json, etc.) that control rendering, validation, and JSON output transformation. The reading path is: (1) the ColumnType base class contract, (2) the plugin hook that registers types and the built-in implementations, (3) the internal DB schema and startup wiring that loads/stores assignments, (4) the Datasette API methods (get/set/remove), (5) the rendering pipeline changes in table/row views, and (6) write-path validation on insert/update, ending with the test suite.

chapters.title: ColumnType base class — the contract every type implements
register_column_types hook spec + built-in url/email/json types
Datasette startup wiring — registering types and loading config
Rendering pipeline — column type render_cell takes priority
Write-path validation on insert, upsert, and update
Documentation — internals API, plugin hook reference, internal DB schema

units.layerReason: Core domain model / contract — everything else builds on this
units.summary: New base class defining the three extension points: render_cell (HTML), validate (write-path), transform_value (JSON output). All return None / pass-through by default, so subclasses only override what they need.

units.layerReason: Plugin contract layer — defines the two hookspecs that the rest of the system relies on
units.summary: render_cell gains a new column_type parameter (passed to plugins so they can inspect it); register_column_types is the new hookspec through which plugins advertise ColumnType subclasses.

units.layerReason: Built-in implementations of the new plugin hook
units.summary: Three built-in column types shipped with Datasette. Each implements render_cell (safe HTML anchor/pre) and validate (regex-based). Note that validate skips None/empty-string values intentionally.

units.layerReason: Wires the new default_column_types module into the plugin manager at module load time
units.summary: datasette.default_column_types added to the DEFAULT_PLUGINS tuple so it is always loaded.

units.layerReason: Persistence layer — the table that stores all assignments
units.summary: Adds the column_types table to the internal SQLite DB, keyed on (database_name, resource_name, column_name). config is stored as JSON text.

units.layerReason: Application startup — populates the in-memory registry and seeds the DB from config
units.summary: _column_types dict is initialized empty in __init__, then populated from the register_column_types hook during invoke_startup (duplicate names raise StartupError). Config loading from datasette.json is deferred until after schemas are refreshed.

units.layerReason: Application-layer API methods that plugins and internal code call
units.summary: _apply_column_types_config walks the datasette.json databases→tables→column_types config and calls set_column_type for each entry (warning on unknown type names). The get/set/remove methods are the stable public API — get_column_type(s) looks up the DB, instantiates the class with its config, and returns a live ColumnType instance with .config populated.

display_columns_and_rows now fetches the ct_map once per call and (a) annotates each column dict with column_type/column_type_config for the display_columns extra, and (b) tries ct.render_cell before falling back to pm.hook.render_cell. The column_type arg is also forwarded to the plugin hook so plugins can inspect it.

units.layerReason: JSON API extra — same priority logic for the render_cell extra
units.summary: The extra_render_cell async function inside table_view_data applies the same ct-first-then-plugin logic for the ?_extra=render_cell JSON output.

units.layerReason: Row detail endpoint — same priority pattern
units.summary: The row endpoint's render_cell extra follows the same pattern: fetch ct_map, try ct.render_cell first, fall through to the plugin hook with column_type= forwarded.

units.layerReason: Query view display_rows doesn't have column type context; passes None
units.summary: The query-view display_rows helper passes column_type=None to render_cell since custom SQL queries have no table-level column type assignments.

units.layerReason: New ?_extra=column_types JSON extra exposed on the table endpoint
units.summary: extra_column_types returns {col_name: {type, config}} for all assigned columns. It is registered in the asyncinject Registry and wired into the extras bundle.

units.layerReason: JSON API — post-processes raw SQLite rows before data['rows'] is set
units.summary: After all extras resolve, each row dict is passed through ct.transform_value for any column with an assigned type before being placed in data['rows']. This is what allows a plugin to normalize values in JSON output.

units.layerReason: Shared validation helper called by all write paths
units.summary: Shared helper that iterates rows, looks up ct_map, and calls ct.validate for each column present in the row. Returns a list of 'col_name: error' strings.
Called after _validate_data passes; if ct_errors is non-empty the view returns a 400 immediately.
Same pattern as insert: validate the update dict against column types before executing the write.
Documents get_column_type, get_column_types, set_column_type, remove_column_type with parameters, return values, and code examples.
Adds column_types DDL to the internal database schema reference.
Documents the new column_type parameter on render_cell and the priority rule (column type wins over plugins).

Full documentation for the new register_column_types hook: example ColorColumnType plugin, class attribute requirements, all three method signatures, per-column config, datasette.yaml config syntax, and the three built-in types.
Adds datasette.default_column_types to the --all plugins output example.

units.symbol: fixture + internal DB / config loading tests
units.summary: ds_ct fixture creates a posts table with email/url/json/markdown column types in config. Tests verify the column_types table is created, config is loaded correctly (unknown type 'markdown' is silently skipped), and the type+config dict form works.
Tests for get_column_type, get_column_types, set/remove round-trips, and config round-trip with a custom config dict.
Verifies built-in types are registered by name and that class attributes (name, description) are correct.
Tests the column_types extra (type/config dict per column) and that display_columns includes column_type/column_type_config fields.
Verifies that the render_cell extra returns href links for url columns, mailto: links for email, and <pre> blocks for json.
Covers all validation scenarios: invalid email/url/json return 400 with column name in error; valid values and null/empty-string values pass through with 201.
Verifies the default implementations of render_cell/validate/transform_value return None/None/value.

units.symbol: render_cell extra and duplicate name tests
units.summary: render_cell extra uses column type rendering; registering a type with a duplicate name raises StartupError.
Row detail endpoint honours column type rendering; transform_value is applied to rows in the JSON output (UpperColumnType test).

units.symbol: Column type priority over render_cell plugins test
units.summary: PriorityColumnType wins over a competing render_cell plugin hook — the column_type arg is passed to plugins so they could inspect it, but if the column type already rendered the value plugins are skipped.
Verifies column type rendering flows through to the HTML row detail and table list pages.

units.symbol: Upsert validation, warning, and config-overwrite tests
units.summary: Upsert validation mirrors insert; unknown type in config logs a WARNING; config always overwrites any manually-set type in the internal DB on startup.
Minimal smoke-test in test_plugins.py ensuring the hook is exercised by the standard hook-coverage test suite.
